message:
fix(tests): correct test expectations and document cancel safety

- choreography/mod.rs: Revert test continuation name from "main" back
  to "x" to match the choreography protocol's expected label; "main"
  was incorrectly introduced in a prior refactor and caused the
  continuation lookup to silently succeed with wrong semantics

- raptorq/decoder.rs: Change test assertion from expect("should solve")
  to unwrap_err() — the test validates that an under-determined system
  correctly fails to decode, so expecting success was the wrong polarity

- runtime/task_handle.rs: Add doc comment clarifying cancel safety
  semantics (abort-on-drop behavior) so callers understand that
  dropping a TaskHandle cancels the underlying task, which is the
  intended design but was previously undocumented

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -3335,7 +3335,7 @@ mod tests {
                     "a",
                     "go",
                     Interaction::comm("a", "data", "Data", "b")
-                        .then(Interaction::continue_("main"))
+                        .then(Interaction::continue_("x"))
                         .expect("comm interactions accept continuations"),
                     Interaction::comm("a", "done", "Done", "b"),
                 ),
